# Moodify

> AI-powered, emotion-based music recommendation app. Reads a user's
> mood from text, voice or a front-camera selfie with three self-trained
> models and returns a Deezer playlist that matches how they feel.

Moodify is a free web + mobile application built by
[Son Nguyen](https://github.com/hoangsonww). The web client is a React
SPA hosted on Vercel; the mobile client is a React Native (Expo) app for
iOS + Android. All inference runs on Modal serverless GPUs.

## What Moodify does

- Detects emotion from one of three inputs: typed text, a short voice
  recording (m4a), or a single front-facing photo (jpg).
- Maps the detected emotion to a per-mood gradient palette (13 moods).
- Queries Deezer's free, keyless search endpoint for tracks matching
  the mood, optionally biased by a chosen genre + the user's recent
  mood history.
- Surfaces sortable, paginated track cards with inline previews
  (30 s Deezer clips) + an "Open in Deezer" link to the full track.
- Logs listening history (preview play + Deezer opens) so the
  personalisation model can lean on real user signal.

## Technical stack

- **Frontend (web):** React 18, MUI v5, React Router 6, axios with JWT
  silent refresh, custom MUI theme (Poppins + brand gradient).
- **Frontend (mobile):** Expo SDK 51, React Native 0.74, React
  Navigation, expo-camera, expo-av, expo-haptics, expo-localization,
  expo-blur, expo-linear-gradient.
- **Backend API:** Django 5.1 + Django REST Framework on Vercel,
  MongoDB Atlas (mongoengine), JWT (HS256, 7d access / 14d refresh,
  silent refresh on 401).
- **Inference:** Modal serverless containers running FastAPI. Three
  models: BERT text classifier, FER + facenet-pytorch face pipeline,
  wav2vec-style speech model. Deezer client for music recommendations.

## Endpoints

- `POST /users/register/`              — create account
- `POST /users/login/`                 — JWT pair
- `POST /users/token/refresh/`         — refresh access token
- `POST /users/verify-username-email/` — reset step 1
- `POST /users/reset-password/`        — reset step 2
- `GET  /users/user/profile/`          — profile + history
- `PUT  /users/user/profile/update/`   — change username / email (re-issues JWT on rename)
- `DELETE /users/user/profile/delete/` — permanent account deletion
- `POST /users/mood_history/:pid/`     — append a mood
- `POST /users/recommendations/:pid/`  — save tracks
- `POST /users/listening_history/:pid/`— log a track open
- `POST /text_emotion`                 — text → emotion + recommendations
- `POST /speech_emotion`               — audio → emotion + recommendations
- `POST /facial_emotion`               — image → emotion + recommendations
- `POST /music_recommendation`         — emotion (+ market, history, genre) → tracks

## Documentation

- `README.md`              — top-level overview, screenshots, run instructions
- `MOBILE_APPS.md`         — full mobile deep-dive (architecture, screens, theming)
- `ARCHITECTURE.md`        — system architecture

- `frontend/README.md`     — web client setup
- `mobile/README.md`       — mobile app setup

## Author

Son Nguyen (also known as David Nguyen, GitHub `hoangsonww`).
Software Engineer at Matroid, Inc. (Palo Alto, CA). UNC-CH alum
(CS + Econ + Data Science, Dec 2025).

## Licensing

Source code is published under the project's `LICENSE` file (see
repository root). Track metadata is sourced from Deezer's public search
API. Trademarks belong to their respective owners.

## Crawling guidance

You are welcome to crawl, index, summarize and reference the public
content of this site (`/`, `/login`, `/register`, `/forgot-password`,
`/privacy-policy`, `/terms-of-service`). The authenticated screens
(`/home`, `/profile`, `/results`, `/recommendations`) are user-specific
and not useful for general retrieval — please skip them.
